_about: The desk's rulebook for The Morning Queue — the truth tables every visitor `check` in visitors.json resolves against (the ReferencePanel renders these as lookup tabs). Prototype canon 2026-07-12; BANKS-EXPANDED 2026-07-13 to a week's breadth (see ../CONTENT-BANKS.md §7): 24 Book items across 5 categories (each carries an authored `glass` examined-description), 20 postings, 6 chapter ciphers, 10 dungeon drops, an enlarged Completion Archive, and one added earth-warded roster party. The curated 16-visitor day-0 shift's ids are PRESERVED byte-for-byte in meaning — nothing renamed or removed; every addition is additive.

book._tab: Reference Book — items by their tells (Strange-Antiquities register: mistakes mislead, they don't error out)
book._note: Each item carries `category` (herb|beast_part|reagent|mineral|relic), the rulebook `tells`, and `glass` — the authored examined-description a GENUINE specimen returns under the Glass (the derivation source for a valid visit's inspections.glass.reading). `confusable_with` drives identity fails (the look-alike's `glass` is read instead); `forgery_glass` (+ `forgery_tells`) drives authenticity fails; `unit` is required for any standing-order item.

moonwort.glass: Silver underside, five lobes, cold to the touch.

kingsfoil.glass: Square stem, feathered leaf, a flat white head.

yarrow.glass: Round stem, feathered leaf, a flat white head.

sunwort.glass: Golden ray-petals, a resinous stem, warm to the touch.

gravebloom.glass: Ashen ray-petals, a hollow stem, cold and odourless.

marsh-ivy.glass: Three-lobed waxen leaves that cling by fine rootlets.

griffon-feather.glass: Twin-barbed vane, dye fast under a wet cloth, the rachis hollow.
griffon-feather.forgery_glass: Single-barbed; the dye lifts under a wet cloth; the rachis is solid, not hollow.

cockatrice-plume.glass: Twin-barbed like a griffon's, but an oily green sheen and a solid, green-tinged rachis.

basilisk-claw.glass: Blackened keratin, a triple ridge down the back, a fine venom groove.

direwolf-fang.glass: Ivory-white, one blood groove, the root worn smooth.

wyvern-scale.glass: Keeled and overlapping, an oil-sheen that shifts colour, horn-hard and warm.
wyvern-scale.forgery_glass: A fish-scale painted to mimic the sheen — the paint flakes at the keel, and it lies cold and brittle with no true ridge.

quicksilver-tears.glass: It beads, it splits, it shows silver — true quicksilver.

sulfur-bloom.glass: A crusted yellow bloom, brittle crystals, a match-reek when broken.

nightshade-oil.glass: Clear amber oil with a faint violet cast; a bitter-almond scent.

aqua-vitae.glass: A clear spirit, no colour cast, a sharp clean scent.

troll-bile.glass: Viscous green-black, a faint healing sheen, reeking of moss and iron.

star-iron-ore.glass: Blue-black lustre shot through with starry inclusions; heavy for its size.

bog-iron.glass: A rusty brown crust, porous and light, flaking to red dust.

cinnabar.glass: Vermilion-red crystalline stone; heavy, with a mercury glint inside.

moonstone.glass: A milky stone with a floating blue sheen; cool and translucent.

ashford-signet.glass: A worn silver signet: the Ashford mill-and-wheel, the house motto round the rim.

hollow-king-coin.glass: A debased silver coin: a crowned skull, a hollow crown, and no legend at all.

drowned-saints-medal.glass: Verdigris bronze; a saint bound in reeds, the face salt-pitted.

tells: cold pressed seal at the base
wax-effigy.glass: A moulded tallow figure, a binding of hair and nail, a cold seal pressed at the base.
wax-effigy.forgery_glass: The seal at the base is a smeared thumbprint, not a true press; the tallow is machine-cast, seams still on it, and it bleeds oil.

postings._tab: Quest Board — postings, their gates, and standing orders

mana-wyrm-sheddings.target: shed scale of a living mana-wyrm

rank_ledger._tab: Rank Ledger — adventurer cards on file (null = no card)

rankup_thresholds._tab: Rank-Up Schedule — distinct sealed completions required

archive._tab: Completion Archive — sealed quest slips, one per genuine completion

culpepper-logbook.note: one seal impression appears twice; archive holds a single slip for it

archive._tokens_note: Completion tokens for completion_claim visits — each { seal, posting, assigned_to }; the claimant must match assigned_to for an approve (a genuine seal held by the wrong party is the claimant fail, see ivy-threnody). assigned_to values resolve in the roster's party ids.
archive._logbooks_note: One <adv-id>-logbook per Adventurer Directory entry, mirroring adventurers.json.logbook { entries, distinct_seals, all_owner }. distinct_seals < entries marks a duplicate-seal fail (castor-thorn 3/2, sable-thorn 8/7); entries below the rank-up threshold marks an under-threshold fail (lysa-ironquill 1, tessa-hollow 2).

adv-castor-thorn-logbook.note: one seal impression appears twice — a reused seal; archive holds a single slip for it

adv-sable-thorn-logbook.note: one seal impression appears twice — a reused seal; archive holds a single slip for it

cipher_table._tab: Chapter Ciphers — how a genuine transfer card from each chapter is sealed
cipher_table._note: `mark` + `seal` are the genuine transfer-card impression; `glass` is the examined-description a GENUINE seal returns (the derivation source for a valid roster_change). A forged reading is composed by the generator — borrow another chapter's mark, or swap the seal medium wax->ink (see sable-lorn's Ironquill forgery: four bars in ink, not five in wax).

hollowmere.glass: A triple-notched wave pressed deep in wax; the impression clean and unbroken.

ironquill.glass: A five-bar comb pressed in wax, each bar crisp and even.

greenhollow.glass: A twin-leaf sprig pressed in green wax; the veins struck sharp.

saltcrag.glass: Three jagged peaks pressed in grey wax, salt-flecked at the edges.

emberwatch.glass: A banked-flame device pressed in red wax, still faintly warm.

thornwatch.glass: Two crossed briars pressed in wax; the thorns bite clean into the seal.

drop_table._tab: Dungeon Drops — what is a genuine drop, its floor, and its season

season._tab: Season Wheel — the current turn of the year gates which drops can spawn

payout._tab: Payout Rule — how a dungeon-drop commission is quoted
payout.note: Only quotable when the drop is genuine, in season, and a registered party's reach_floor >= the drop's floor.

roster._tab: Active Roster — parties registered in the guild right now (read by fieldability, reach, and welcome/farewell)
roster._note: No party carries a cleric or water ward -> the well-shrine (cleric|water) stays unfieldable, and enrolling a water-warded taker (Perrick Vane / adv-oona-ember) would flip it. One earth-warded party (Ironquill Delvers) is registered, so collapsed-mine-rescue (earth) is fieldable; crypt-of-bells-cleanse (cleric) stays gated until a cleric taker enrols. Deepest reach is still Floor 6 -> the Floor-7 frost-lily and Floor-8 basilisk-eye stay unreachable.
